compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body:
Bug-sweep investigation (root cause NOT yet confirmed — recording evidence, no fix shipped):

REFUTED hypothesis: 'detached background gc (gc.autoDetach=true) races the commit'. Experiment: after the test's 36-ticket churn a fresh store holds only 154 loose objects, far below git's gc.auto=6700 threshold; 'git gc --auto' does nothing and no gc.log appears. So no background gc fires during this test — the gc/index.lock story is disproven.

NOT reproducible locally: 0/50 isolated runs, 0/25 whole-file runs, and 0/200 runs under 8-way CPU saturation. No shared-path cross-test race vector found either: the HLC clock lives per-clone in .rebar/ beside each tracker, and the /tmp/.ticket-sync-<md5> marker is keyed per store — so separate temp stores don't contend even under parallel CI. (pytest-xdist is not a declared dep, so local runs are serial.)

VERIFIED facts: both CI signatures are git add/commit failing against the tickets worktree. The transition path (txn.py:94) already surfaces git's real stderr ('fatal: could not parse HEAD'); the CREATE path (event_append.py:139) SWALLOWS add.stderr behind the generic 'git commit failed while holding lock', so create-path failures hide their true cause.

RECOMMENDED next step to CONFIRM (not done here): surface the swallowed git stderr at event_append.py:139 (mirror txn.py:94's '{cp.stderr}') so the next CI occurrence reveals the real create-path cause; that observability change is diagnostic, NOT a confirmed fix. Root cause of 'could not parse HEAD' on the tickets worktree under CI load remains open. Leaving this ticket in_progress.

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Diagnosability change up for review: Gerrit 369 (fix(store): surface git's real stderr on create-path commit failure). event_append.py's create path now includes git's stderr instead of hiding it behind the generic 'git commit failed while holding lock', so the next CI occurrence reveals the true cause (matching the transition path). This is NOT the flake fix — root cause still unconfirmed (not locally reproducible; 0/275 repros + gc hypothesis experimentally refuted). A background flaky-test sweep (6x full suite) is running to try to reproduce it.

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Flaky-detection sweep result (6 serial full-suite runs, ~14min each): 6/6 CLEAN — 4660 passed, 0 failed/errored on every run. vocal-dip-robin did NOT reproduce in any serial full-suite run (nor in the earlier 0/50 isolated + 0/25 file-level + 0/200 saturated loops). This reinforces that the git-race is specific to the CI runner's conditions (concurrency/load/filesystem), not serial execution — so it is not locally reproducible. No OTHER flaky tests surfaced across the 6 runs (no additional bugs to file). Status: root cause still UNCONFIRMED; the landed diagnostic (change 369, event_append.py now surfaces the real git stderr on the create path) will expose the true cause on the next real CI occurrence, at which point this can be driven to a confirmed root cause + fix. Leaving open pending that CI evidence.

compiled_state.description:
## Summary
`tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_transition_open_children.py::test_guard_counts_only_direct_children_not_unrelated`
is FLAKY in CI: it passes locally and on re-run, but intermittently fails with a git-state race
in its temporary rebar-store fixture. Observed repeatedly while landing epic sure-foyer-aroma's
13-change Gerrit stack — different changes' CI runs failed on this one test, each cleared by a
`recheck`, and it passes deterministically when run locally in isolation.

## Reproduction Steps
1. Push a stack of changes to Gerrit (or otherwise trigger the full default CI suite repeatedly).
2. Observe that on some runs this single test fails during its temp-store setup, while 4500+ other
   tests pass; a `recheck` (same patchset, no code change) then passes.
Two distinct failure signatures observed (both git races in the fixture's temp repo):
- Run 28825842708 (change 325, ps2): `rebar._errors.RebarError: rebar transition failed (exit 2):
  Error: git operation failed: fatal: could not parse HEAD`
- Run 28828139721 (change 339): `rebar._errors.RebarError: rebar create failed (exit 1):
  Error: git commit failed while holding lock`

## Expected vs Actual
- **Expected:** the test is deterministic — it passes on every run given identical code (it passes
  locally 100% of the time and on CI rechecks).
- **Actual:** it fails intermittently in CI (~1 in N runs) with a git error (`could not parse HEAD`
  / `git commit failed while holding lock`) originating in the temp rebar-store setup, not in the
  behavior under test (the direct-children guard count). The failure is environment/timing-driven.

## Likely Root Cause (hypothesis — confirm before fixing)
The test's fixture builds a temp rebar store and performs `create`/`transition` against it. The two
signatures suggest either (a) an operation running before the store's git HEAD/initial commit is
fully established (`could not parse HEAD`), or (b) `index.lock` contention — a prior git op's lock
not yet released, or concurrent access under xdist/parallel CI. Investigate the fixture's
store-init ordering and any lock-release/retry gap in the `git commit`/`create` path used under
test. This is a TEST/fixture-reliability bug, not a defect in the transition-guard behavior itself.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Root cause of the intermittent git race in the fixture is identified (HEAD-not-ready vs
      index.lock contention) and documented.
- [ ] The fixture (or the store-init/commit path it exercises) is made deterministic — e.g. ensure
      the initial commit/HEAD exists before ops, and/or the lock acquisition retries/serializes —
      so the test cannot fail on this race.
- [ ] The test passes reliably under repeated/parallel CI runs (no `recheck`-only greens); ideally
      add a stress/repeat guard or a regression note so the flake does not silently return.
compiled_state.title: Flaky test_guard_counts_only_direct_children_not_unrelated: intermittent git-race (could not parse HEAD / index.lock) in temp-store fixture
